title: Reconciling the seeming disparity of the Ending
display_title: Reconciling the seeming disparity of the Ending
wikitext:
[]: Reconciling the seeming disparity of the Ending             Quote Post by bigboss90210 » Sun Aug 29, 2021 4:21 pm Hello all, Lurker for awhile here and the ending really made me want to throw out some ideas here to see what you guys would say. First of Just wanna say that 3+1 was such an amazing movie and loved every second of it. Ok so the ending. I feel like the main way to decipher the ending at least is thru the lens of Christopher Nolan's Inception. Also if somebody has said this before totally whoops but i haven't seen it yet. Before going into Rei's quantum tunnel in the cockpit of Unit 08 Mari says two really important things. First she say to get Asuka out. She is still down there. Second she says to wait for her no matter long it takes. Later Gendo says that the anti-universe is basically incomprehensible and that the lcl digs into ur memories to make it understandable and tangible. Basically analogous to the concept in Inception to being in the subconscious. So when Shinji gets his super confidence he makes it a point to free, Asuka, Kaworu, and Rei. Since all three of them plus Shinji were in the eva in one way or another they were all stuck in Gendo/Shinji's memories/perception/subconscious. Asuka gets freed then later appears at KenKens house so basically she's good. Kaworu gets saved and gets to hang out with Kaji and then later with Rei at the Train station. Ok and Rei is long haired and has the Tsubame doll so i bet its just standard issue Rei soul splitting/reunifying stuff. We later see her at the train station too so its all good there too. Now here is the part where the Inception stuff comes in. Shinji was going to sacrifice himself but is moved and saved by Yui who threw him towards the water's surface. Yui and Gendo get to embrace and self-sacrifice. They impale themselves with the Gaius spear and remove evas except for 08. Yui and Mari being old friends I can totally see this as having been planned out. Then we see Shinji on the beach vibing out and the animation progressively degrades. This part i take as Shinji in limbo. Stuck in his own subconscious. The animation degrading i take as him being there a very long time. Months or years even. Thats why normally aloof Mari makes it a point to tell Shinji to wait. And since Shinji is stuck in his thoughts IN the anti-universe I bet time-wimey effects come into play. Shinji had to wait the long way. And Mari in complaint ready Unit 08 is probably unaffected by the double whammy of time dilation. Sort of how in inception going down deeper makes time go slower. So in this way the ending with the crew making it down and seed ships and Asuka at KenKens is what happens. The way that it zooms in on Giga-Rei breaking into pieces and into the people and the cows and the cats is the real ending. Everyone lands is, is written into a world probably similar to our present day, plus the survivors from the old world having already been there get mixed in. The villages and survivors just smirk at each other and are too grateful for the new world to say anything and just everyone starts rebuilding. So that in this way Maya and Farmer Ritsuko art that's going around is probably cannon. And the world goes on. Mari looks for Shinji but is protected from time effects really affecting her by Mark 08. But Shinji is hit by the double whammy of being in the anti-universe AND being inside the Golgotha Object/his sub-conscious(memories). It probably takes Mari hours or a couple days to make it to Shinji. But its been A LOOOOONNNNG time for Shinji. Probably long enough to catch up in age to the rest of the pilots. still not sure. But def older. Definitely months, probably years. But since this is at peace Shinji and Mari told him to wait he simply waits even tho it does to start wearing on him. He still okay just it awhile. Near the end he does slightly seem to falter per the degradation in the animation. Thats why Mari says something like not a moment too soo or something. Mari gets out into the water and Unit 08 blinks out of existence probably under Yui's wishes. Then Mari and Shinji finally get added back to world we saw earlier just later because they've been temporally unavailable. long enough for the world to be fixed if by the people if Giga-Rei didn't fix it herself. So thats how I see the ending. Would love anyones thoughts on this. Basically the two ending are the same ending it just took awhile for Mari to get Shinji out and Shinji was under double time dilation effects. Thanks y'all!

[]: Re: Reconciling the seeming disparity of the Ending             Quote Post by Bryshe » Sun Aug 29, 2021 9:02 pm google translate Shinji has aged because the curse of Eve has disappeared, and not from a long stay in the Anti-Universe.

[]: Re: Reconciling the seeming disparity of the Ending             Quote Post by bigboss90210 » Sun Aug 29, 2021 9:12 pm hmm i wasn't really trying to address the Shinji aging but more like how the some details would seem to indicate that the two parts of the ending make it seem like two separate endings. i was just saying how its one ending and Shinji and mari come back into it along time after its been reset.
